summary: I read both design guides (`backend-guide.md`, `ui-style-guide.md`) and the iteration-2 Define-phase artifacts under review — the business requirements (R1–R33), open questions, and discovery notes — then checked the specs against the guides and wrote my verdict to `verdict.json`. **Verdict: pass.** The deliverables are deliberately non-technical business requirements with no code, design, or diff, so the code-structural guide rules (service/Result interface, thin controllers, no logic in callbacks/jobs, Tailwind token scale, semantic status colors, smallest-DOM-unit Turbo targeting, Minitest) have nothing to bind against yet and correctly defer to the design/build phases. Where the specs do touch guide-governed surfaces they actively align — R4 and R25 encode the UI guide's "label, not color alone" a11y rule, R30 enforces plain non-technical language, and the discovery notes map every proposed capability onto existing service POROs rather than callbacks or fat controllers. I noted one non-blocking observation (R15's "restart in progress" state doesn't restate the not-color-alone rule that R4/R25 do, though the principle is already established doc-wide), so findings are empty and guide alignment becomes substantively re-checkable once a design/diff exists downstream.
